title: Garlicky Smoked Kenyan Fish Tagine
description: A hearty, garlicky stew from Kenya that's perfect for a cold morning or afternoon. Made with smoked fish, amazake, and binchotan-charcoal.

ingredients.name: Onion
ingredients.notes: Chop the onion into small pieces

ingredients.name: Amazake
ingredients.notes: You can use plain yogurt or milk as a substitute

ingredients.name: Binchotan charcoal
ingredients.notes: You can find binchotan charcoal at most Japanese markets

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika is optional but delicious

instructions: Heat the binchotan charcoal in a small grill or grill pan until it starts to smoke.
Add the minced garlic to the grill and let it cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped onion to the grill and let it cook for 2-3 minutes, until softened.
Add the grated ginger to the grill and let it cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked fish to the grill and break it up into small pieces.
Add the cumin, coriander, and paprika to the grill and stir to combine.
Pour in the amazake and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the stew cook for 10-15 minutes, until the fish is cooked through.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped cilantro or scallions if desired.
